CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-09-2026 Off Calendar

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Jan-08-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Prior to Open Court, Atty. Jim O'Brien appears via zoom in an baundance of caution. Counsel is released at 9:05am. 9:19am Glenn Egrie appears via Zoom. The court did not receive any notice to contest the court's TR.

No appearances in Open Court. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

1 - Defendant Anita Washington's Motion to Quash Service of Summons is GRANTED.

(see word minutes for full details)

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Jan-08-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Prior to Open Court, Atty. Jim O'Brien appears via zoom in an baundance of caution. Counsel is released at 9:05am. 9:19am Glenn Egrie appears via Zoom. The court did not receive any notice to contest the court's TR.

No appearances in Open Court. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

2 - Defendant Bey & Associates, LLC's Motion to Quash Service of Summons is GRANTED.

(see word minutes for full details)

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-09-2025 Continued To Feb-25-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Nov-10-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

1 - DEFENDANT ANITA WASHINGTON, BEY AND ASSOCIATES, LLC MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS.

Defendant Anita Washington's Motion to Quash Service of Summons or Stay or Dismiss is OFF CALENDAR.

Moving Defendant's proof of service is not properly sworn and, thus, of no evidentiary value. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 2015.5.) Thus, Moving Defendant did not timely file proper proof of service of the moving papers. (See California Rules of Court, rule 3.1300(c).) Additionally, Moving Defendant neither timely filed nor timely served their moving papers. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 1005(b) [motion must be filed and served 16 court days before hearing]; Code of Civil Procedure section 1013(a) [service by mail where place of address or place of mailing is outside State of California but within United Stated adds ten calendar days to "any period of notice"].) see also Code of Civil Procedure section 1010.6(a)(3)(B) [service by email adds two court days to "any period of notice"].)

Moving Defendant must prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing.

No proposed order received. Moving party failed to provide and follow the court's order.

Counsel for the moving defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Nov-10-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Moving Defendant's proof of service is not properly sworn and, thus, of no evidentiary value. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 2015.5.) Thus, Moving Defendant did not timely file proper proof of service of the moving papers. (See California Rules of Court, rule 3.1300(c).) Additionally, Moving Defendant neither timely filed nor timely served their moving papers. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 1005(b) [motion must be filed and served 16 court days before hearing]; Code of Civil Procedure section 1013(a) [service by mail where place of address or place of mailing is outside State of California but within United Stated adds ten calendar days to "any period of notice"].) see also Code of Civil Procedure section 1010.6(a)(3)(B) [service by email adds two court days to "any period of notice"].)

Moving Defendant must prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
